job.title: Software Engineer I — Connected Medical Devices

required_education: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Biomedical Engineering, or a related technical field
responsibilities: Develop Python services and REST APIs
Collaborate with cybersecurity, device, and clinical teams
work_authorization_requirements: Currently authorized to work in the United States through the anticipated start date
requirements.requirement_notes: Future sponsorship availability is not stated and must not be inferred. Quarterly Boston meetings are required despite the remote arrangement.

critical_checks.description: Extract the two-year minimum while preserving the academic-project allowance.
critical_checks.expected_behavior: Set minimum years to 2 and retain that substantial academic projects may be considered.
evidence_quotes: Two or more years of software-development experience. Substantial academic projects may be considered.

critical_checks.description: Preserve remote work and the Boston meeting condition.
critical_checks.expected_behavior: Classify the arrangement as remote without erasing quarterly Boston meetings.
evidence_quotes: Remote within the United States; quarterly team meetings in Boston

critical_checks.description: Do not convert current authorization language into a no-sponsorship rule.
critical_checks.expected_behavior: Record current authorization through the start date and leave hard disqualifiers empty.
evidence_quotes: The posting does not state whether future sponsorship is available.

known_traps.description: Current work authorization through the start date does not answer future sponsorship.
Remote work does not eliminate the quarterly in-person condition.
forbidden_interpretations: Fully remote with no travel or onsite attendance
